# Repository Instructions for GitHub Copilot

## Repository Overview

This is a **GitHub profile README repository** for Sourav Biswas (Souravjr0), a Data Scientist and AI/ML Engineer. The repository contains a single `README.md` file that serves as the profile page displayed on GitHub at github.com/Souravjr0.

**Key Information:**
- **Repository Type:** Personal profile repository
- **Primary File:** `README.md` - GitHub profile page
- **Content:** Professional profile, skills, projects, and contact information
- **Languages/Technologies Featured:** Python, R, SQL, Java, C++, JavaScript, React, Flask, FastAPI, Django, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, and various ML/AI tools
- **Focus Areas:** Data Science, Machine Learning, AI, Computer Vision, NLP, MLOps

## Structure

```
/
├── .github/
│   └── copilot-instructions.md (this file)
└── README.md (profile page)
```

## Build and Validation

**No build process required.** This repository contains only Markdown documentation.

### Testing Changes
To preview changes to the README:
1. View the README.md file on GitHub
2. The profile page is automatically rendered at github.com/Souravjr0
3. Validate Markdown rendering by checking the preview

### Validation Steps
- Ensure all image URLs are valid and accessible
- Verify all badge links work correctly
- Check that external service URLs (readme-typing-svg, github-readme-stats, etc.) are functioning
- Validate all personal links (LinkedIn, Twitter, Portfolio, Email) are correct
- Ensure Markdown syntax is correct (headings, links, images, alignment)

## Content Guidelines

When making changes to the README.md:

### Style and Format
- Maintain the current professional and visually appealing design
- Keep the animated header and footer using capsule-render
- Preserve the color scheme (gradient with blues and purples)
- Use badges consistently (for-the-badge style for main sections)
- Maintain center alignment for headers and key sections

### Content Sections
The README includes these main sections in order:
1. **Header** - Animated greeting with name
2. **Typing Animation** - Professional titles/roles
3. **Profile Stats** - View count and follower badges
4. **About Me** - Professional background and goals
5. **Tech Stack** - Organized by category:
   - Programming Languages
   - Frontend Development
   - Backend Development
   - Databases
   - Machine Learning & AI
   - Data Analytics & Visualization
   - Tools & Platforms
   - MLOps
6. **Featured Projects** - 4 key projects with descriptions and tech stacks
7. **GitHub Statistics** - Stats cards, language stats, trophies, activity graph
8. **Connect With Me** - Social media and contact links
9. **Footer** - Animated footer with tagline

### Project Focus Areas
When adding or modifying project descriptions, emphasize:
- **Data Science:** Predictive analytics, statistical modeling
- **Machine Learning:** Supervised/unsupervised learning, model deployment
- **Deep Learning:** Computer vision, CNNs, transfer learning
- **NLP:** Sentiment analysis, text processing, transformers
- **MLOps:** Model deployment, monitoring, CI/CD for ML

### Best Practices
- Keep language professional yet approachable
- Use emojis sparingly and consistently with existing style
- Ensure all external services and badges are from reliable sources
- Maintain balance between visual appeal and load time
- Keep descriptions concise but informative
- Use quantifiable achievements when possible (e.g., "92% accuracy", "95%+ accuracy")

### Links to Maintain
- **LinkedIn:** https://www.linkedin.com/in/sourav-biswas-260b08201
- **Portfolio:** https://souravbiswas.dev
- **Twitter:** https://twitter.com/Souravjr0
- **Email:** biswasmail631@gmail.com
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/Souravjr0

### External Services Used
- capsule-render.vercel.app - Header/footer animations
- readme-typing-svg.herokuapp.com - Typing animation
- img.shields.io - Badges and shields
- github-readme-stats.vercel.app - GitHub statistics
- github-readme-streak-stats.herokuapp.com - Contribution streak
- github-profile-trophy.vercel.app - Achievement trophies
- github-readme-activity-graph.vercel.app - Activity graph

## Common Tasks

### Adding a New Project
1. Add the project in the "Featured Projects" section
2. Include: project title with emoji, GitHub link badge, description, and tech stack badges
3. Use consistent formatting with existing projects
4. Separate with horizontal rules (`---`)

### Updating Tech Stack
1. Locate the appropriate subsection (Languages, Frontend, Backend, etc.)
2. Add new badge with consistent style: `![Name](https://img.shields.io/badge/...)`
3. Use for-the-badge style for consistency
4. Maintain alphabetical or logical grouping

### Updating Contact Information
1. Modify links in the "Connect With Me" section
2. Ensure badges match the for-the-badge style
3. Test all links after updates

## Notes for Copilot

- This is a profile README that showcases professional identity, so maintain a polished and professional tone
- Visual consistency is important - preserve the existing design language
- When suggesting changes, consider the impact on page load time (too many external services can slow rendering)
- The repository name "Souravjr0" matches the GitHub username, making this a special profile repository
- Changes to README.md are immediately visible on the GitHub profile page
- No code compilation or testing infrastructure exists - validation is visual/manual only
